title: Add 'editorial naturalness' dimension to G-Eval framework — baseline pass on the 10 AUT-7 lifted skills
protocol: EVAL_FRAMEWORK.md Section A — Editorial Naturalness rubric (1-5 anchored on observable lexical, structural, tonal, and genre tells).
evaluator: Claude (EditorialLead agent, AUT-23 heartbeat) under CEO direction recorded in AUT-23 comment 12196048.
calibration_methodology: Each skill was scored against its in-skill canonical example output. SKILL_TEMPLATE.md (post-AUT-23) explicitly designates the example as the dimension reviewer's primary calibration target — if the example reads like AI, every output the skill produces will too. This is a single-output baseline pass: a full 3-input re-eval against varied inputs is a follow-up that should be triggered for any skill scoring 3 or below on this dimension. None of the ten skills triggered that follow-up: all scored 5/5.
scope_note_aut7_sync: The pre-existing .evals.json files for these 10 skills were never updated by the AUT-7 actionability relift (commit 040690a) — they still reflect the original pre-AUT-7 dimension means and per-test-case scores, even though the public eval_score in the skill frontmatter and the EVAL_SUMMARY.md table both reflect post-AUT-7 values. AUT-23 does NOT rewrite the per-test-case scoring history (which is a record of the original eval session and should stay). Instead, AUT-23 adds an editorial_naturalness_baseline top-level block to each .evals.json containing the AUT-23 scoring evidence, and updates the frontmatter eval_score to the new 7-dimension mean computed from the AUT-7 after numbers (recorded in tests/runs/AUT-7-actionability-relift.json) plus the new Editorial Naturalness score. EVAL_SUMMARY.md is the public truth of record and is updated in this commit to match.
threshold_rule: Per CEO direction (AUT-23 comment 12196048): stable now requires (overall mean ≥ 4.0 across 7 dimensions) AND (Editorial Naturalness ≥ 4.0 individually as a hard floor). Demotion policy if > 5 fall below the floor: pause and escalate. ≤ 5: demote and open per-skill lift-editorial-naturalness child issues without further check-in. Outcome: zero demotions — all 10 skills cleared the hard floor at 5/5.

skills.tells_observed: None. Verbs are direct ('flag,' 'verify,' 'pull'). Hedge language is technical and earned ('confidence interval,' 'requires verification'). 'Skyrocketed' / 'exploded' appear only in quoted source material being checked, not in the output's own voice.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The Issue Type taxonomy (Likely Accurate / Context Missing / Methodology Concern / Possible Misquote / Misleading Framing / High Risk) is exactly the working vocabulary a magazine fact-checking desk uses. The Next Step note ('resolve statistic 2 first ... then run claim-verification-checklist') is a real working handoff, not a boilerplate close.
skills.status_decision: stable (passes both bars: overall mean 4.60 ≥ 4.0 AND editorial_naturalness 5.0 ≥ 4.0 hard floor)

skills.tells_observed: None. Status labels (CONFIRMED / SINGLE SOURCE / UNVERIFIED / DATE APPROXIMATE) are domain jargon, not filler. Em-dash use is functional, not parenthetical-in-every-sentence.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The 'Internal Working Version — Not for Publication' subtitle is exactly the register marker a working investigations editor uses. Each gap entry is a populated remediation template with concrete custodians ('city clerk's office,' 'state corporation registry') and named owners — directly assignable as reporting tasks.

skills.tells_observed: None. Latinate verbs that appear ('substantiate,' 'corroborate,' 'adjudicate') are legal terms of art, correctly used. 'Significantly' appears in legal-register technical context ('substantially more plaintiff-friendly').
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The Red/Amber/Green tiering with per-passage Legal Concern + Recommended Action + Suggested Rewrite is the standard structure of an in-house counsel pre-publication note. The closing disclaimer ('This brief is an editorial pre-screening tool. It is not a substitute for review by a qualified media attorney.') is the kind of italicised note a real media lawyer would write.

skills.tells_observed: None. Phrasing is vivid and specific ('a woman who has made her peace — or believes she has,' 'the dam is her work problem, not yet her emotional problem'). No restate-the-question intro, no boilerplate close.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The film's central question is articulated in a single line that earns its place: 'can you repair something if you are partly made of the forces that broke it?' That is genuine documentary thinking, not AI summarising. The translation rules ('one arc moment ≠ one shoot day,' 'reactive blocks need standby crew, not scheduled crew') are real working principles a producer would internalise.

skills.tells_observed: Borderline only: 'The tone is neither elegiac nor polemical' is a controlled antithesis but earns its place by rejecting two specific common documentary modes the bible is positioning against. Not counted as a tell.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The bible shifts register accurately between sections — punchy logline, substantive overview, broadcaster-specific commissioning vocabulary that uses each broadcaster culture's actual editorial language (PBS public-interest mandate, Channel 4 distinctive-voice register, ARTE in French). That register agility is the genre craft commissioning editors look for.

skills.tells_observed: None. The colloquial parenthetical '(these are gold for later interviews)' is appropriate to the 'professional, conversational register' the spec calls for, not a sycophantic break.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: Doc director's working voice is correctly inhabited — observational and specific ('His working life was built entirely within a single institution before it disappeared beneath him at 42,' 'the first day's job is to make the second day possible'). The 8-step first-session plan distinguishes camera and no-camera moments correctly, which is the practical knowledge a working director carries.

skills.tells_observed: None. The Editorial Verdict line ('an advocate first and a researcher second') is a real, specific judgment, not hedge fog.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: Neutral analytical voice held throughout — no editorialising about the source's character, but a clear weight assignment based on observable factors (institutional mission, funding, methodology). The Follow-Up Questions are tailored with the source's actual claims and organization name, which is the operational standard fact-checking desks expect.

skills.tells_observed: None. Imperative voice is direct and operational ('Come with data,' 'Lead with the systemic question, not the specific hospital, to lower defensiveness').
skills.strongest_genre_craft: Working editor's voice — short sentences, named platforms, specific search filters. The Sourcing Risks section names real failure modes ('advocacy capture,' 'overreliance on government sources') without abstracting them. Reads like a memo from a senior investigations editor briefing a junior reporter.

skills.tells_observed: None. Cost-range qualification is technical and quantitative without being padded — every figure carries per-unit basis, rights window, territory, and year as the spec requires.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: The 'Archive availability for marginalized communities is systematically worse than for mainstream or institutional subjects' line is a real, concrete editorial observation — a working archive researcher's earned knowledge, not AI generality. The category-3 fallback plan ('if no moving-image footage is locatable, shift to stills + audio reconstruction') is the kind of contingency thinking that separates a brief from a wish list.

skills.tells_observed: None. The Location Function paragraph uses vivid, specific imagery ('a working environment that no longer works,' 'the physical argument for the community's sense of displacement') without crossing into purple prose.
skills.strongest_genre_craft: Site Visit Checklist items are physically verifiable on location ('Time it carrying a 60-pound case,' 'Sit silently in the turbine hall for 5 minutes') rather than abstract ('assess accessibility'). That move from abstraction to verifiable action is the genre craft of a line producer's brief.

next_actions: Post the demotion list (in this case: empty) on AUT-23 for CEO record before AUT-23 is closed.
Hand off Section B prose review to BrandCopywriter as a follow-up child issue (non-blocking — the framework commit landed, BrandCopywriter reviews the public-facing prose after).
Future full-library Editorial Naturalness pass should evaluate the remaining 219 stable skills. That is a separate scope decision for the next eval round, not in AUT-23.
summary.calibration_observation: The 10 AUT-7 lifted skills were already constructed with editorial care — examples are vivid, registers inhabit the genre, no AI-template phrasing. This is partly a selection effect (AUT-7 was a careful relift round on already-stable skills), so a 10/10 pass is not evidence the wider library will perform identically. The next round's signal will come from category breadth, not from these 10.
